mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exhibit a persistent upward trend over time, reflecting underlying economic expansion. This secular growth in employment drives consumer income and spending, supporting corporate earnings and pushing S&P 500 prices higher over monthly horizons.
failure_condition: Rolling 24-month rank correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and subsequent 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03, or PAYEMS declines while SPX still produces positive returns in >30% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real purchasing power and raises the probability of central bank tightening, even when the federal funds rate is at zero. This expectation of future rate hikes depresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%, the SPX 60-day return is positive more than 55% of the time over any 10-year period, or the negative signal has a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero and consumer price inflation rises, the real discount rate for equities becomes increasingly negative, creating a distortion that compresses risk premiums. The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and stable FEDFUNDS indicates a monetary regime that fails to respond to inflation, undermining real yields and leading to negative forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase > 0.3% and FEDFUNDS unchanged at ≤ 0.10%
regime_hint: zero lower bound with incipient inflation
failure_condition: In instances where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM and FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.10%, the SPX 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction regression coefficient is not statistically significant at the 5% level over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate aftermath of a severe economic shock, a sharp rebound in real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) signals a V-shaped recovery in aggregate demand. This early recovery phase is characterized by rapid inventory restocking and consumer spending, which quickly translates into higher corporate profits and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: During any post-recession window (NBER recession end + 12 months), the month-over-month growth rate of RSAFS has a rank IC of ≤ 0.02 with subsequent 5-day SPX returns, or the positive return prediction fails more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic output. A sustained increase in GDPC1 drives aggregate corporate earnings higher over long horizons, and the market prices this growth with a lag. The strong positive trend in GDPC1 supports further S&P 500 gains over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The quarterly growth rate of GDPC1 has a negative rank correlation with SPX 60-day forward returns over any 20-year period, or the forward return during quarters of positive GDP growth is not significantly larger than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tightening labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and wage income. When UNRATE falls below a key psychological threshold (5.5%), it confirms that the labor market has healed sufficiently to support sustained consumption, leading to positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE drops below 5.5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not statistically significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture the health of the manufacturing and construction sectors. When both series rise concurrently, it signals broad-based industrial expansion and robust investment demand, which drives earnings growth in cyclical industries and pushes the S&P 500 higher over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both record positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: In months where both INDPRO and HOUST rise, the SPX 20-day return is positive less than 55% of the time, or the average return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (ZLB), conventional monetary accommodation is exhausted and the level of FEDFUNDS loses its signaling power about future tightening. In this regime, the absolute level of FEDFUNDS (near zero) paradoxically encourages risk-taking through a 'reach for yield' channel, supporting short-term equity prices. The mechanism is active only under ZLB conditions.
failure_condition: During ZLB periods (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.10%), the rank IC of FEDFUNDS level with subsequent 5-day SPX returns exceeds 0.02, or the average 5-day return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
